Background:
thumb|left|Kawaki living with his father.
As a child, he lived alone with his drunken, abusive father, Kokatsu. In the anime, he was forced to chop wood for a living in order to buy alcohol for Kokatsu. One day, a goldfish merchant invited him to a goldfish show that he was holding the next day after Kawaki had shown interest in the goldfish. Later, his father noticed the invitation in his pocket and punished Kawaki for interacting with strangers by forbidding him dinner. The next day, Kawaki was beaten by two boys until the goldfish merchant saved him and offered him a job by helping him sell goldfish in other villages. Kawaki hurried home after noticing the crack in the glass of his father's alcohol. Giving nothing but broken glass, Kawaki was locked in a room by Kokatsu. 
Shortly after, Jigen arrived to propose buying him with a large sum of money. Not wanting to leave his home, Kawaki promised his father that he could do more work to earn money but was knocked unconscious when Kokatsu smashed an empty bottle on his head. Upon taking custody of the boy, Jigen told Kawaki that the suffering he endured was over and there was nothing to fear, as he was his new father. As Kawaki and Jigen took the only tunnel out of the village, the seemingly friendly merchant from before revealed himself as a child trafficker, hoping to take Kawaki for himself. After Kawaki had waited outside the tunnel for the two to settle who would take the boy, Jigen came out, claiming the man had "gladly withdrawn".
thumb|Kawaki being subjected to Jigen's experiment.
When he was first brought to Kara, Jigen took Kawaki to Amado's laboratory, telling him that he would give Kawaki a gift when they truly become parent and child. 
As part of a ritual, Kawaki was one of fifteen Ōtsutsuki vessel candidates that were placed in artificial wombs connected by tubes to jars of fluid, through which Jigen attempted to transfer his Kāma to them. The first thirteen children all died, however, Jigen succeeded with Kawaki, one of his last attempts. Kawaki later awoke with a Kāma branded on his left hand. Despite Jigen's promise of safety, Kawaki would later describe his life from then on to be a living hell.
Sometime after the Ōtsutsuki ritual, Kawaki was attacked by Code, who was so jealous that he alone proved viable as Isshiki's vessel that he came to openly hate Kawaki, making it clear that if he wasn't a vessel, he'd kill Kawaki with his own hands.
Personality:
Stemming from his harsh childhood, Kawaki is shown to be aggressive and vulgar to people he dislikes or opposes. At the same time, despite his efforts to distance himself from others, he was not completely heartless. He showed sympathy for weak and injured animals, and refused to let innocent children get hurt. Also, he can remain calm and collected when questioning strangers, such as when Kawaki listened to Konohamaru Sarutobi's explanation about himself and his team. When not letting his temper get the best of him, Kawaki is shown to be very observant and pragmatic, easily reading the flow of battle before him and how best to react. Likewise, he is capable of seeing through deceptions and quickly formulating a cunning thought-out plan. Mitsuki considers him reliably effective in conveying his feelings, even if done in an aggressive manner.
Due to the massive suffering he has endured while forced to be a vessel of the Kara, which he described as being a living hell, he would gladly die than be stuck in said hell for the rest of his life. He has even shown signs of post-traumatic stress disorder, as simply seeing dripping roots of plants serves as a reminder of his struggles and has left him panting in horror. He appears to loathe himself for what he has become over the years he spent with Kara, stating that he would learn the Shadow Clone Technique just so he could beat himself up to vent out.
Kawaki is known to hold grudges, especially towards Kara, becoming highly alert and aggressive when facing off against members of the group. He particularly resents Jigen for what he deemed as a violation of his promises of safety, as shown by how he quickly erupted into a rage while unconscious upon being reminded of Jigen's adoption of him and later on when he showed clear resentment while describing Jigen's experiment. However, while appearing to be confident in his ability to defeat the Outers, he knows better than to believe than he could possibly come out on top against any of the Inners, knowing of their completely monstrous might, and has shown fear of them, sweating in the presence of Delta and expressing brief but great shock that Naruto could be more powerful than even Jigen, who he knew full well was the strongest member of Kara.
Abilities:
Having been modified into an Ōtsutsuki-cyborg and trained by Jigen from a young age, Kawaki is a remarkably powerful fighter. With the power he wields, Kawaki could easily defeat dozens of Kara’s robotic puppets, as well as one of their Outer members, Garō, who was personally selected by the Inner member Delta to recapture him. Even the rest of the Kara Inners have openly praised his might. He has even been hypothesised to be an incredible shinobi if he could learn to use ninjutsu along with his scientific enhancements. Upon inheriting Isshiki's power and abilities, he could contend with a Boruto-possessed Momoshiki, then later easily overpower a restrained Code. Soon after, it is thought that nobody in Konoha, including the Seventh Hokage, is able to oppose his power. Three years later, it is believed that Amado adjusted Kawaki's battle settings to be just enough to fight against the full-powered Code. Though once Amado fully adjusted Kawaki's settings to be fully offensive, he proves powerful enough to be able to briefly compete against the God Tree Leader, Jura. Upon further upgrades, Kawaki is able to easily overpower dozens of Human God Trees at once.
[Kāma] thumb|left|Kawaki's Kāma.
Kawaki possesses the Kāma of Isshiki Ōtsutsuki, which he was originally branded with to be his new vessel. Having proved compatible with the mark, Kawaki's body has gradually been rewritten with Isshiki's genetic data. With it, he gains access to Isshiki's combat experience, the ability to absorb ninjutsu, and can open space-time portals to other dimensions. He can even channel the Kāma’s power into his weapons to release powerful short range blasts. As the mark continued to advance, Kawaki began subconsciously accessing a transformed state, manifesting a small horn on the left side of his head. Kawaki's contact with Boruto, who bears Momoshiki's Kāma, created a resonance that accelerated the takeover rate of both vessels. Soon after, Isshiki's forced resurrection would lead to Kawaki’s Kāma being erased, though his genetic data had already been rewritten with mostly Ōtsutsuki data. 
thumb|Kawaki's initial Kokugan.
Amado would later restore the Kāma, now functioning only as a weapon with Isshiki's soul gone. Soon after, the Kāma advanced enough for Kawaki to access Isshiki's Kokugan in his left eye. This granted him access to various shinjutsu, such as Sukunahikona which he can use to shrink and return to normal size himself or any nonliving matter.
